title: Multiweight tangential operators
summary: Defines multi-input tangentiality and weight mapping, packages both properties, and proves closure of tangentiality under sums and scalar multiples.

mathematicalContent.heading: Multi-input tangentiality
mathematicalContent.body: For homogeneous tuples F and G with the same weighted boundary values in every slot, a rank-r operator P is tangential at w when P(F) and P(G) have the same boundary value.

mathematicalContent.heading: Weight mapping
mathematicalContent.body: P maps input multiweight w to output weight wout if the output is homogeneous of weight wout whenever every input Fᵢ is homogeneous of weight wᵢ.

mathematicalContent.heading: Packaged operators and closure
mathematicalContent.body: WeightedMultiTangentialOperator stores the weights, operator, mapping theorem, and tangentiality theorem. Raw tangentiality is closed under addition and scalar multiplication; packaged closure additionally requires an output-homogeneity proof supplied by the caller.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines tangentiality at a multiweight: changing any homogeneous input by a weighted Q-error must not change the output boundary value.
correspondence.leanType: def IsTangentialAtWeights {r : ℕ} (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) (w : Fin r → ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the mapping of an input weight vector w to one output homogeneity wout.
correspondence.leanType: def MapsWeights {r : ℕ} (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) (w : Fin r → ℝ) (wout : ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Packages input weights, an output weight, a rank-r operator, a proof of weight mapping, and a proof of tangentiality at those input weights.
Abbreviates the weighted tangential package at rank two.
Abbreviates the weighted tangential package at rank three.
Proves raw tangentiality of P+Q when P and Q are both tangential at the same input multiweight.
correspondence.leanType: theorem add {r : ℕ} {P Q : CalConf.MultiOperator r} {w : Fin r → ℝ} (hP : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights P w) (hQ : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights Q w) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (CalConf.multiAdd P Q) w

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves raw tangentiality of a scalar multiple cP from tangentiality of P.
correspondence.leanType: theorem smul {r : ℕ} {P : CalConf.MultiOperator r} {w : Fin r → ℝ} (c : ℝ) (hP : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights P w) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (CalConf.multiSmul c P) w

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves raw tangentiality of P+Q when P and Q are both tangential at the same input multiweight.
correspondence.leanType: def add {r : ℕ} (P Q : CalConf.WeightedMultiTangentialOperator r) (hinput : P.inputWeights = Q.inputWeights) (_houtput : P.outputWeight = Q.outputWeight) (hmaps : CalConf.MapsWeights (CalConf.multiAdd P.toOperator Q.toOperator) P.inputWeights P.outputWeight) : CalConf.WeightedMultiTangentialOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves raw tangentiality of a scalar multiple cP from tangentiality of P.
correspondence.leanType: def smul {r : ℕ} (c : ℝ) (P : CalConf.WeightedMultiTangentialOperator r) (hmaps : CalConf.MapsWeights (CalConf.multiSmul c P.toOperator) P.inputWeights P.outputWeight) : CalConf.WeightedMultiTangentialOperator r

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: Tangentiality closure under addition and scalar multiplication is proved from the unary modulo-Q laws. For packaged operators, output homogeneity of sums and scalar multiples is not derived: the caller supplies hmaps. The add constructor also accepts an output-weight equality argument but does not use it computationally.
